feedback.text: I am writing to express the need of extend the Digital Fairness Act to encompass the space of digital entertaining (AKA videogames), as many have highlighted with initiatives like the Stop Killing Games, there's a strong need to enforce stronger measures to protect consumers to lose what they paid for and assert the ownership of the products, not just some "license". Also important to establish rules for payment processors to not be able to deny transactions on platforms unless they have effectively and unequivocally flagged as having illegal practices. Current event like VISA to force platforms like itch.io or Steam to have to remove content under the threat of being denied usage because of some "undetermined" risks is not acceptable and attempts against consumers.
